description_long: This is a single-bladed axe with a robust, slightly curved wooden handle and a heavy, wedge-shaped metal head. The blade is broad and flat, with a sharp edge that appears slightly worn and stained, possibly from use. The metal head shows a mottled texture with areas of discoloration, including hints of rust and darkened spots, as well as some yellow and red stains. The handle is made of dark, natural wood, featuring visible grain patterns, knots, and a smooth, ergonomic curve for grip. The axe head is securely mounted onto the handle in a traditional style, with no visible fasteners or modern attachments. There are no visible brand markings or labels, but the overall design suggests a utilitarian tool suitable for chopping wood or similar tasks in an urban or suburban environment.
description: A weathered, single-bladed axe with a curved wooden handle and a stained, textured metal head. The tool shows signs of use and age, with visible wood grain and discoloration on the blade.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the axe, highlighting the curved wooden handle and the broad, stained blade with visible texture.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the narrow edge of the blade and the straight alignment of the handle beneath it.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, but with different lighting and visible stains on the blade and handle.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the axe head and the handle, with the poll of the blade visible and the handle's end in shadow.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with visible grain, ergonomic shape, oval cross-section, smooth but untextured grip.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad wedge-shaped blade with a straight edge, no beard, flat poll, and visible wear and stains.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted onto the handle.
